entities.name: Ngern Tid Lor Public Company Limited

financial.note: BAY holds ~46.51% equity as of April 2026; provides critical funding synergy and access to a lower cost of funds compared to smaller peers.
Holds ~3.28% equity; provides institutional stability and long-term capital backing within the SET50 index.
Holds ~0.43% equity; reflects steady domestic institutional confidence in the non-bank financial sector.

supply_chain.note: Internal insurance brokerage wing; No. 2 retail non-life broker in Thailand. High-margin fee income acts as a buffer against lending margin compression.
Primary source for vehicle title loan leads; 2026 expansion relies on deeper integration with local auto and motorcycle dealer networks.
Branch utility costs; 2026 energy price reductions provide a minor operational tailwind for its 1,700+ location network.

competitors.note: Direct rival for the vehicle title loan market; MTC's aggressive provincial branch expansion pressures TIDLOR's urban-digital strategy.
Competition for motorcycle and car title market share; TIDLOR differentiates through its 'Tidlor Card' revolving credit feature.
Rivalry in the personal loan and title-loan-to-card segment (KTC P BERM vs Tidlor Card).

macro_commodity_factors.logic: BoT rate levels dictate cost of funds; TIDLOR benefits from a high proportion of fixed-rate debentures and Krungsri's financial support.
Directly correlates with the repayment capability of rural customers; 2026 price stability helps lower Expected Credit Loss (ECL) provisions.
High systemic debt (~87-90% in 2026) increases credit risk; TIDLOR's prudent loan approval (NPL target <3%) is critical for asset quality.
